title: Fix failing unit tests
body: These changes fix the failing tests introduced by https://github.com/PrefectHQ/prefect/pull/18450

step_plan.summary: Fixes unit test regressions introduced by recent version-inference changes by making Prefect’s VCS/version metadata inference more robust (string-vs-enum handling, empty git repos, missing remotes, and missing CI env vars). The deploy CLI’s downstream behavior becomes stable in common test setups like temporary directories and minimal git initialization.

steps.operation: Harden git commit message extraction to handle empty/no-commit repositories and git failures gracefully
steps.reason: Unit tests commonly create temporary git repos (sometimes with `git init` but no commits). If `git log -1` yields no output or errors, the current `splitlines()[0]`-style logic can raise (IndexError/CalledProcessError), causing deploy/version inference tests to fail nondeterministically.
steps.side_effects: May change behavior from raising to returning `None`/empty string (depending on existing return contract). Any callers/tests asserting an exception will need updates; downstream version inference may now proceed further instead of crashing.

steps.operation: Normalize `version_type` inputs and fix enum comparisons to ensure correct inference for both strings and `VersionType` values
steps.reason: Regression likely stems from comparing a string-typed `version_type` to an enum member using identity (`is`), which will never match when callers pass string values (e.g. from CLI args/config). Converting `version_type` to `VersionType` (when possible) and using equality/robust mapping prevents incorrect branching and spurious `Unknown version type` errors in deploy-related tests.
steps.side_effects: Could alter which getter runs for a given input and change error semantics (e.g., from ValueError to fallback/None). Might require minor alignment in CLI deploy behavior and tests if they relied on previous (buggy) branching.

steps.operation: Make git-based version inference resilient when git metadata is missing (no remote, not a repo, git not installed) and return consistent, catchable failure signals
steps.reason: Tests often run outside real repositories or with minimal git setup (no `origin`, detached HEAD, shallow data). This helper should not raise unexpected exceptions mid-way; it should either return `None` (so inference can continue to other sources) or raise a consistent exception that `get_inferred_version_info` can handle predictably.
steps.side_effects: Downstream commands (notably CLI deploy) may now omit git fields instead of failing. Some tests may need to adjust expected version info content (e.g., absent remote URL / repo URL) if they previously depended on exceptions or partial outputs.

steps.operation: Validate GitHub environment inputs before constructing repo/commit URLs to avoid malformed URLs in test environments
steps.reason: In unit tests, CI environment variables like `GITHUB_SERVER_URL` may be unset, leading to malformed URLs (e.g. `None/<repo>/tree/<sha>`). Adding guards (require server URL + repository + sha) and returning `None`/partial info consistently avoids failing assertions and keeps inference behavior aligned with other providers.
steps.side_effects: GitHub-derived version info may become `None` more often in local/test runs, shifting inference to git/local/simple strategies. Any tests asserting URL construction with missing env vars will need updates, but behavior will be more predictable across environments.
